Bug title: Invite link immediately redirects unauthenticated users to homepage
Expected result: Unauthenticated user sees the join form, fills in their details, and successfully joins the community.
Actual result: The join page flashes briefly then the user is immediately redirected to the Kyozo homepage. New members cannot accept the invite or join the community.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Admin sends a WhatsApp or email invite from Audience > Invite Member.
2. Recipient opens the invite link while not logged in.
3. Observe page behaviour.

Bug title: Private community toggle has no effect — community always created as Public
Notes: Confirmed via screenshots: a community named 'test private community' (created with toggle ON) displays the same 'Public' badge as 'test public community' (created with toggle OFF). The toggle UI renders correctly but the value is not being passed through on creation.
Expected result: Community is created as Private when the 'Private community' toggle is enabled
Actual result: Community is always created as Public regardless of the toggle state. The toggle appears to have no effect on the community's visibility setting.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Navigate to the community creation page
2. Enable the 'Private community' toggle
3. Fill in the community name and any required fields
4. Submit / create the community
5. View the newly created community

Bug title: Bulk CSV import extremely slow — ~17 min for 1,001 rows (sequential API calls)
Notes: Progress indicator UI works correctly (spinner + % counter + 'do not close' message). Test was aborted after ~25 seconds at 2% to avoid polluting test data. A small number of rows (User141, User142) were partially imported before cancellation. Root cause is likely N+1 API calls instead of a batch endpoint.
Expected result: Import completes within a few seconds using batch API calls; progress bar advances quickly to 100%
Actual result: Progress bar advances approximately 1% per 10 seconds, projecting ~17 minutes total for 1,001 records. The importer appears to be making one sequential API call per contact rather than batching.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Go to a community's Audience tab on http://pro.kyozo.com
2. Click Import Members → CSV File
3. Upload a CSV with 1,001 rows of valid name/email data
4. Click "Import 1001 Members"
5. Observe the progress bar

Bug title: Eventbrite import creates duplicate audience entries for attendees with multiple ticket types
Notes:
Confirmed during happy path testing of Eventbrite import on community t1. Duplicates observed: mailto:testuser9@gmail.com (x2), mailto:testuser11@gmail.com (x2), mailto:testuser2@gmail.com (x2). The ticket type is stored in the Notes column in the preview — deduplication by email with merged notes would be the expected behaviour.

Follow-up check on member profiles confirmed each duplicate Audience row results in a separate community membership record. The 'Communities' stat on each affected profile shows 2, with 'Member of 2 Communities' listing t1 @t1 twice. This elevates the bug to Critical — duplicate memberships cause real data integrity issues beyond cosmetic duplication.
Expected result: Each unique attendee (identified by email address) should appear as a single audience entry. If they purchased multiple ticket types, this could be represented via tags or notes, but they should not be duplicated as separate members.
Actual result:
Attendees who purchased multiple ticket types are imported as separate, duplicate audience entries — one per ticket type. For example, an attendee registered for both 'General Admission' and 'Second category' appears twice in the audience with the same name and email. In a test with 16 Eventbrite rows (13 unique attendees), all 16 rows were imported, resulting in 3 duplicate audience entries.

Further investigation confirmed the impact goes beyond the Audience list view: each duplicate row creates a genuine duplicate community membership. Affected profiles (testuser2, testuser9, testuser11) all show 'Member of 2 Communities' — both entries being the exact same community (t1 @t1). This means duplicated users will receive broadcasts twice, count twice in member stats, and appear twice in guestlists.
Steps to reproduce:
1. In http://pro.kyozo.com, open a community and click Import Members.
2. Select Eventbrite and enter a valid Private API Token.
3. Click Load Events and select an event that has at least one attendee registered under multiple ticket types.
4. Click Fetch Attendees — observe the preview table shows the same attendee (same name + email) on multiple rows, one per ticket type.
5. Click Import 16 Members.
6. After import completes, check the Audience list.

Bug title: TSV import — tab delimiter not recognised, all row data repeated in every column
Page/Feature: Audience → Automatically Integrate → CSV / TSV importer (Preview step)
Notes: Test file had a proper header row (Name, Email, Phone, Tags) and three data rows, all tab-separated. The importer appears to only handle comma delimiters and does not parse TSV files despite advertising .tsv support. The import does not fail gracefully — it silently saves malformed records to the database. Confirmed by inspecting the audience list post-import: Diana Prince, Ethan Hunt, and Fiona Green all have their full concatenated row string stored as name, email, and tag. Severity kept at High (no Critical option) but this warrants urgent attention.
Expected result: Each contact's fields are displayed in the correct columns: Name → NAME, Email → EMAIL, Phone → PHONE, Tags → TAGS
Actual result: Tab delimiter is not recognised. Every column (NAME, EMAIL, PHONE, TAGS) shows all four fields of the row concatenated as a multi-line block in the preview. If the user proceeds to import, the corrupted data is saved to the database: each contact's Name, Email, and Tags fields all receive the full concatenated row string (e.g. name = 'Diana Prince mailto:diana@example.com +15550103 vip', email = 'Diana Prince mailto:diana@example.com +15550103 vip', tag = 'Diana Prince mailto:diana@example.com +15550103 vip'). The Phone field is left empty. Results in completely unusable audience records with no warning shown to the user.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Open the Audience page of a freshly-created community
2. Click 'Automatically Integrate'
3. Choose 'CSV File (.csv / .tsv)'
4. Upload a valid .tsv file whose columns are Name, Email, Phone, Tags separated by tab characters
5. Click Next and inspect the Preview screen

Bug title: Custom Handle slug not auto-generated from Community Name for any input
Notes: Initially suspected to be a non-Latin character issue (Cyrillic / special chars). Re-tested with a real user typing plain Latin text ("Hello World") — the slug still did not auto-generate. The auto-generation is broken for all inputs. The field stays empty with no error or warning, and the form allows proceeding to Step 2 with no handle. When the handle is empty, the backend falls back to using the raw community name verbatim as the URL slug (see KYPRO-61).
Expected result: The Custom Handle should auto-populate with a URL-safe slug derived from the community name (e.g. "Hello World" → "hello-world")
Actual result: The Custom Handle field remains empty for all tested inputs. The URL hint stays as "http://pro.kyozo.com/your-handle" with no generated slug. No error or warning is shown and the form proceeds to Step 2 with an empty handle.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Open Create Community modal (fresh)
2. Type any community name in the Community Name field (tested with Latin "Hello World", Cyrillic "клавдий тестирует", and all-special-chars "!@#$%^&*()")
3. Tab or click away to another field
4. Observe the Custom Handle field and the URL hint below it

Bug title: Community with raw-name URL slug is inaccessible after creation — crash or "Community not found" depending on character type
Notes:
Three distinct failure modes observed:

1. NON-LATIN UNICODE (Cyrillic, Chinese — likely all Unicode scripts):
   Examples: "клавдий тестирует" → /клавдий-тестирует, "你好" → /你好
   Browser navigates to percent-encoded URL (e.g. /%E4%BD%A0%E5%A5%BD)
   Result: no crash, sidebar shell loads with community name visible, but main content shows "Community not found"
   Community is inaccessible

2. SPECIAL CHARACTERS containing URI-breaking chars (%, #, ?, etc.):
   Example: "!@#$%^&*()+=[]{}|;':"",./<>?`~"
   Result: full app crash — "Application error: a client-side exception"
   Console: URIError: URI malformed at decodeURIComponent
   App must be manually reloaded

3. PARTIAL SPECIAL CHARACTERS (chars that don’t break URI parsing):
   Examples: "test-new!", "[test-:)]"
   These navigate without crashing but communities may still be inaccessible

Root cause: empty handle (see KYPRO-60) causes backend to store raw community name as slug. Pattern is consistent across all tested non-Latin scripts.
Expected result: The community should either not be created with an invalid URL, or a safe fallback slug should be used. Navigating to the community should work without errors
Actual result: The backend saves the raw community name verbatim as the URL slug (e.g. href="/!@#$%^&*()+=[]{}|;':",.<>?/`~"). Clicking the community card triggers a full application crash: "Application error: a client-side exception has occurred" with console error: URIError: URI malformed at decodeURIComponent
Steps to reproduce:
1. Create a community whose name contains only characters that produce an empty URL slug (e.g. "!@#$%^&*()+=[]{}|;':",.<>?/`~" or Cyrillic text)
2. Community is created successfully (see KYPRO-60)
3. Click on the community card from the Communities list

Bug title: Background image upload fails silently during Create Community — image not applied
Notes: File tested: ~7.5MB jpg image, within the stated 10MB limit. An error message appeared indicating the image could not be loaded. The form allowed the user to proceed and the community was created, but the background image was not saved. The community header shows a plain grey gradient instead of the uploaded image.
Expected result: Background image uploads successfully and is displayed in the community header banner
Actual result: An error message appears when uploading the background image. Despite the error, the form allows the user to continue and the community is created, but the background image is not saved — the header shows a plain grey gradient instead
Steps to reproduce:
1. Open Create Community modal
2. Complete Step 1 (Basic Info) and click Next
3. On Step 2 (Customization), upload a background image within the 10MB limit (tested with ~7.5MB)
4. Observe the error message
5. Proceed and click Finish
6. Open the created community

Bug title: Plan limit reached error only shown after user completes the full 2-step Create Community form
Notes: Reproducible when plan limit of 5 communities is reached. System should detect the plan limit before the modal opens, not after the user fills both steps.
Expected result: The system should detect the plan limit before the user starts the flow — either by disabling the Create Community button with a tooltip, or showing a blocking message when the modal opens
Actual result: A toast "Plan Limit Reached – You've reached your plan limit of 5 communities. Upgrade your plan to create more." only appears after the user fills out both steps and clicks Finish, wasting significant user effort
Steps to reproduce:
1. Have an account that has reached the plan limit (5 communities)
2. Click the Create Community button on the Communities page
3. Fill out all fields in Step 1 (Basic Info) and click Next
4. Fill out fields in Step 2 (Customization) and click Finish

Bug title: Password reset link from http://pro.kyozo.com redirects to http://www.kyozo.com (member-facing app) instead of the admin panel
Notes: The reset link URL observed: https://www.kyozo.com/reset-password?mode=resetPassword&oobCode=...&lang=en. The mismatch between http://pro.kyozo.com and http://www.kyozo.com means the reset flow sends admins to the wrong application entirely.
Expected result: The reset link should open a password reset form on http://pro.kyozo.com (the admin panel)
Actual result: The reset link redirects to http://www.kyozo.com (the member-facing frontend) at the path /reset-password. The page title shown is 'Willer - Welcome to the http://Willer.fm community', indicating a community member page rather than an admin password reset form. Admins/owners initiated the reset from http://pro.kyozo.com and should be returned there.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Go to http://pro.kyozo.com and click Sign In
2. Click 'Forgot password?' and enter mailto:ana@kyozo.com
3. Click 'Send reset link'
4. Open the password reset email and click the reset link

Bug title: Member selection state is lost after closing the 'Tag Members' modal — visual state and internal state become out of sync
Notes: The misleading visual state (checkbox + counter showing 1 selected) makes this particularly confusing — the user believes their selection is intact but cannot proceed. Related to KYPRO-51 (X button not working), but reproduced via Cancel button.
Expected result: The 'Tag Members' modal should open with the previously selected member still selected, as the UI indicates they are
Actual result: After closing the modal, the visual selection state appears intact (member row is highlighted, checkbox is checked, counter says '1 of 20 selected') but the internal state is cleared. The 'Add Tags' button no longer shows a count '(1)'. Clicking 'Add Tags' again triggers a browser alert: 'Please select one or more members to apply tags.' The UI and internal state are out of sync.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Navigate to the Audience tab
2. Select a member (e.g. Frank Validuser) — toolbar shows 'Add Tags (1)' and '1 of 20 selected'
3. Click 'Add Tags (1)' to open the Tag Members modal
4. Close the modal using the Cancel button
5. Observe the UI — member still appears checked and '1 of 20 selected' is still shown
6. Click 'Add Tags' again

Bug title: 'Select All' only selects currently loaded members, not all members in the community
Notes: Critical data integrity risk. An admin could believe they are selecting all members to broadcast or tag, but only a small subset would be affected. Behavior changes depending on session state (what has been previously loaded or searched).
Expected result: 'Select All' should always select all members in the community (3016), regardless of what has been previously loaded or searched
Actual result: On initial page load, 'Select All' only selected 20 of 3016 members ('20 of 20 selected'). After performing the 'Test' search (which causes the system to load more member data) and clearing it, clicking 'Select All' correctly selected all 3016 members ('3016 of 3016 selected'). The selection count is tied to how many records are currently loaded in the frontend, not the actual total member count.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Navigate to the Audience tab (community has 3016 members)
2. Click 'Select All' without performing any search
3. Note the number of selected members
4. Deselect All
5. Type 'Test' in the search bar and wait for all 4 results to fully load (~50 seconds)
6. Clear the search
7. Click 'Select All' again
8. Note the number of selected members

Bug title: Validation and API error messages use native browser window.alert() instead of in-app UI components
Expected result: Errors should be shown as inline field validation messages or in-app toast notifications, consistent with the rest of the app UI
Actual result: Native browser window.alert() dialogs are used, which block the entire page, are visually inconsistent, and cannot be styled or dismissed gracefully
Steps to reproduce:
1. Open Invite Member modal and click 'Send Invite' without selecting Email or WhatsApp → native alert fires
2. On the Audience list, click 'Add Tags' without selecting any member → native alert fires

Bug title: Add Member form – all validation errors hidden below the fold
Notes: During QA testing this bug caused the Add Member button to appear broken across multiple test cases. High severity because it directly blocks users from creating members without knowing they need to scroll down. Three distinct error messages are affected: phone required, name required, invalid email.
Expected result: Validation errors should appear immediately adjacent to the relevant field (inline), or at minimum at the top of the form — always visible without scrolling. Required fields should be marked with an asterisk (*) or a "required" label.
Actual result: All validation error messages render below the Profile Banner upload section, which is below the visible dialog area without scrolling. The Add Member button appears completely non-functional to users. Additionally, required fields (Last Name, Phone) have no asterisk or "required" indicator, so users cannot know these fields are mandatory before attempting submission.
Steps to reproduce:
Scenario A – missing phone:
1. Click Add Member in the Audience header
2. Fill First Name, Last Name, valid Email; leave Phone blank
3. Click Add Member
4. Nothing visible happens → scroll down past Profile Banner
5. Error appears: "Phone number is required."

Scenario B – missing last name:
1. Click Add Member; fill First Name, Email, Phone; leave Last Name blank
2. Click Add Member → scroll down
3. Error appears: "First and last names are required."

Scenario C – invalid email:
1. Click Add Member; fill First Name, Last Name, Phone; enter "notavalidemail" in Email
2. Click Add Member → scroll down
3. Error appears: "A valid email address is required."

Bug title: Drag & drop file upload non-functional across all import upload zones (Integrations & Audience)
Page/Feature: Integrations > Import (CSV & Excel) + Audience > Automatically Integrate > Upload CSV/TSV File
Notes: Confirmed systemic — affects all upload drop zones in the app, not isolated to one flow. During QA session April 2026, all file uploads were performed via 'Click to browse' as a workaround because drag & drop consistently failed. Drag & drop in Audience modal reproduced twice consecutively with zero response. Likely a shared drag-event handler registration issue at the component or framework level.
Expected result: Dragging a file over any upload drop zone should trigger a visible hover/active state. Dropping a valid file should register it, show a filename confirmation, and advance the flow. Both 'Click to browse' and 'drag & drop' are explicitly advertised on every drop zone and both must work.
Actual result: Drag & drop is non-functional in all file upload drop zones across the app. The drop zone shows no hover highlight when a file is dragged over it, no visual response on drop, no filename confirmation, no loading indicator, and no error message. Users who attempted to upload files during QA testing had to use the 'Click to browse' file picker instead in all cases. This affects both the Integrations > Import wizard (CSV and Excel flows) and the Audience > Automatically Integrate modal.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Go to Integrations > Import and select CSV File or Excel/XLS as source
2. Drag a valid file from your computer and drop it onto the dashed upload zone
3. Observe — file is not registered
4. Repeat via Audience > Automatically Integrate > CSV source
5. Drag a valid .csv file onto the drop zone
6. Observe — file is not registered

Bug title: Manual Entry accepts and imports contacts with name only — email not enforced as required
Notes: This creates unusable contacts in the system with no email address for communication.
Expected result: Email should be a required field in Manual Entry. The form should display a validation error and prevent submission when email is blank.
Actual result: Contact is accepted and imported with name only. No validation error or warning is shown about the missing email address.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Go to Integrations > Import > Manual Entry
2. Enter a value in the Name field only
3. Leave Email, Phone, and Tags blank
4. Click Add Member
5. Click Import

Bug title: CSV import silently accepts non-comma delimiters, corrupting all data
Notes: Tested with semicolon-delimited file.
Expected result: Importer should detect non-standard delimiters and either: (a) auto-detect the delimiter and parse correctly, or (b) show a clear error asking the user to re-export with comma delimiters.
Actual result: Import completes without error. The entire row including delimiters is treated as a single field value. All data is corrupted (e.g. 'John;mailto:john@email.com;555-1234' imported as a single name field).
Steps to reproduce:
1. Go to Integrations > Import
2. Select CSV as source
3. Upload a CSV file that uses a non-comma delimiter (e.g. semicolons: 'name;email;phone')
4. Proceed through the import wizard to completion

Bug title: Excel import with no header row consumes first data row as headers, silently losing all data
Notes: Tested with a 5-row data-only sheet — only 4 contacts imported, first row used as field names.
Expected result: Importer should ask the user whether the first row contains headers or data. If no header row, the user should be able to define column mappings manually. A warning should be shown if the presumed header row looks like data.
Actual result: The first data row is silently consumed and used as column headers. All remaining rows are imported but the first contact is permanently lost with no warning.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Go to Integrations > Import
2. Select Excel as source
3. Upload an .xlsx file where the first row contains data (not column headers)
4. Complete the import

Bug title: CSV import silently accepts file with missing required columns (name/email)
Notes: Tested with a CSV containing only a 'phone' column — import completed with 0 usable records.
Expected result: Importer should detect missing required columns (name, email) and display a clear validation error before allowing the user to proceed. Import should be blocked until the mapping is corrected.
Actual result: Import completes without error. No warning or validation is shown about missing required fields. Contacts are created with empty required fields.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Go to Integrations > Import
2. Select CSV as source
3. Upload a CSV file that is missing required columns (e.g. no 'email' or 'name' column)
4. Proceed through the import wizard to completion

Bug title: Invalid email accepted in Manual Entry import without any validation error
Notes: Same email validation gap as KYPRO-14 (Audience Add Member). Could result in large batches of invalid emails being imported.
Expected result: Validation error shown on email field: 'Please enter a valid email address'. Import should not proceed with invalid email.
Actual result: Invalid email 'not-an-email' is accepted. Member is added to the preview list. 'Import 1' button is enabled with no warning.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Navigate to Integrations tab
2. Select 'Manual Entry'
3. Enter Name: 'QA Test', Email: 'not-an-email' (invalid format)
4. Click 'Add Member'
5. Click 'Preview (1)'

Bug title: Excel/XLS import fails to parse valid .xlsx file — feature is broken
Notes: The feature lists .xlsx/.xls as supported formats but cannot parse them. The fallback suggestion to 'export as CSV' confirms the feature is non-functional. Tested with a valid openpyxl-generated .xlsx file.
Expected result: File parsed correctly; member data displayed in Preview & Edit step
Actual result: Error toast: 'Could not parse Excel file. Try exporting as CSV.' The Excel import feature fails entirely for standard .xlsx files.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Navigate to Integrations tab
2. Select 'Excel / XLS'
3. Upload a standard .xlsx file (e.g., generated by openpyxl or exported from Microsoft Excel)
4. Observe result

Bug title: CSV import silently accepts unrecognized column headers, causing silent data loss
Notes: Critical data integrity issue. A user migrating data from another system with different column naming would silently lose entire columns of data.
Expected result: Warning identifying unrecognized columns; column mapping UI allowing user to match source columns to target fields
Actual result: Import silently proceeds. 'first_name' partially maps to Name (only first token 'John'); 'last_name' is silently dropped ('Doe' lost); 'telephone' maps to Phone with no warning. User has no indication data was lost.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Navigate to Integrations > CSV File
2. Upload a CSV with non-standard headers: first_name, last_name, telephone
3. Proceed to Preview step and observe parsed data

Bug title: Create Event button stays disabled with partial time input — no user feedback provided
Notes: Blocks event creation entirely. Chrome native time input behavior makes this particularly severe.
Expected result: Validation error or tooltip explaining what is missing/wrong; Create Event button becomes enabled when all required fields are correctly filled
Actual result: Create Event button remains disabled. No error message, tooltip, or any indication of why. The Chrome native time picker is difficult to interact with, leaving users stranded.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Navigate to Schedule tab
2. Click 'Create Event'
3. Fill in event name
4. Attempt to type time values in start/end time fields (partially or incorrectly)
5. Observe Create Event button state

Bug title: 'Guestlist Details' link in guestlist modal is non-functional (dead link)
Notes: Blocks access to guestlist management settings.
Expected result: Navigates to full guestlist detail/management page
Actual result: Link is dead — clicking does nothing
Steps to reproduce:
1. Navigate to RSVP & Guestlist tab
2. Click an existing guestlist to open detail modal
3. Click 'Guestlist Details' link inside the modal

Bug title: Invalid email format accepted without validation in Add Member modal (Audience)
Notes: Data integrity risk. Same issue also found in Integrations Manual Entry (KYPRO-26).
Expected result: Validation error: 'Please enter a valid email address'
Actual result: Member is added with email 'notanemail' — no validation error displayed
Steps to reproduce:
1. Navigate to Audience tab
2. Click 'Add Member'
3. Enter Name: 'Test User', Email: 'notanemail', Phone: '+1-555-0101'
4. Click 'Add Member'

Bug title: WhatsApp invite accepts any text as phone number — no format validation
Expected result: Validation error shown — phone number must be in a valid international format (digits + country code).
Actual result: Invite is dispatched without error. The invalid text is embedded in the WhatsApp share URL (phone=notaphone), which will fail silently for the recipient.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Open Invite Member modal.
2. Select WhatsApp channel.
3. Enter invalid text (e.g. "notaphone") in the Phone Number field.
4. Fill in First Name and Last Name.
5. Click Send Invite.

Bug title: Invite landing page — "Join" button text has poor contrast
Expected result: Button label is clearly legible — sufficient contrast between text and background colour.
Actual result: Button text is a dark/muted shade on a dark purple background, making it nearly invisible. Fails WCAG contrast requirements.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Open Invite Member modal in Audience page.
2. Send a WhatsApp invite.
3. Open the generated invite link.
4. Observe the "Join [Community Name]" button.

Bug title: Signing into a http://kyozo.com community page as an existing user does not enroll the user in the community audience
Notes: Confirmed with mailto:ana+hulkuser@kyozo.com signing into http://kyozo.com/qa-test-community-new — user was absent from the QA Test Community New audience. Contrast: ana+kyozouser who signed UP on http://kyozo.com/ashok-kyozo was automatically added to Ashok@Kyozo's audience. Related to KYPRO-68: despite not being in the audience, ana+hulkuser still gained full admin access to http://pro.kyozo.com after being manually imported — the enrollment bug compounds the auth boundary issue.
Expected result: Authenticating on a community's public page (sign-in or sign-up) should enroll the user as a member of that community. The user should appear in the Audience list in http://pro.kyozo.com immediately after signing in.
Actual result: When an existing http://kyozo.com account signs IN (as opposed to signing UP) on a community page, the user is NOT added to that community's audience. The user remains invisible to the community admin and must be manually imported to appear in the Audience list. This behaviour is inconsistent with the sign-up flow, where a new user who signs up on a community page IS automatically added to the audience.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Ensure a http://kyozo.com account already exists (e.g. mailto:ana+hulkuser@kyozo.com).
2. Navigate to a http://kyozo.com community page (e.g. http://kyozo.com/qa-test-community-new).
3. Click Sign In (not Sign Up) and log in with the existing account credentials.
4. In http://pro.kyozo.com, open the same community and check the Audience list.
5. Observe that the signed-in user does not appear in the audience.

Bug title: No character length limit on Name or Email fields in Manual Entry — extremely long strings accepted and stored
Notes:
No maxlength attribute is enforced on the Name or Email inputs. This is a systemic gap across the Manual Entry form. Very long strings also cause downstream UI layout failures (see KYPRO-67). Could also present database storage risks depending on column type and size constraints server-side.

[2026-04-14 TC7 update] Same overflow also reproduced in the CSV Import preview table. A 510-char name pushes all other columns (Email, Phone, Tags, Notes) completely off-screen with no truncation or horizontal scroll. Affects Integrations > Import > CSV File preview step.
Expected result: Fields should enforce a reasonable maximum length (e.g. 100–255 chars for name, 254 chars for email per RFC 5321). Exceeding the limit should show an inline validation error and prevent submission.
Actual result: Both fields accept and store strings of any length without any limit or error. A 370+ character name and a 500+ character email were successfully imported and persisted to the member record with no warning.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Navigate to Audience > Import Members > Manual Entry
2. In the Name field, type a string of 370+ characters (e.g. 370 x 'A' followed by 'long name test')
3. In the Email field, type an extremely long email (e.g. 250 x 'a' + '@' + 250 x 'a' + '.com')
4. Click + Add
5. Click 'Import 1 Members'

Bug title: Extremely long name or email causes horizontal overflow and broken layout in preview table and member profile
Page/Feature: Audience > Import Members > Manual Entry / Member Profile
Notes: Root cause of long strings being storable is tracked separately in KYPRO-66. Both surfaces (preview table and profile card) are missing CSS overflow handling (e.g. overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap or word-break: break-word). The preview table issue also makes the edit/delete buttons unreachable without scrolling, which is an accessibility concern.
Expected result: Long text should be truncated with an ellipsis (…) within its column/cell. The table layout should remain stable. The member profile card should wrap or truncate long name/email values within the card boundary.
Actual result:
Preview table (step 3): The table overflows horizontally. The long name forces all columns to the right — EMAIL, PHONE, TAGS, NOTES and the ✏️/🗑️ action buttons become inaccessible without horizontal scrolling. The column header row also misaligns and disappears from view during scroll.

Member profile card (step 5): The name and email strings overflow outside the card container horizontally, breaking the profile layout. Text is not wrapped or truncated.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Navigate to Audience > Import Members > Manual Entry
2. Enter a name of 370+ characters and a valid email, click + Add
3. Observe the preview table
4. Import the contact and click on the resulting member in the Audience list
5. Observe the member profile card

Bug title: Phone field in Manual Entry preview edit mode accepts alphabetic characters without validation
Notes: No phone format validation exists anywhere in the Manual Entry flow — neither in the Add Member form nor in the inline edit mode of the preview table. Same pattern as email validation gaps (KYPRO-37) and empty field validation gaps (KYPRO-34). Likely a systemic absence of input validation across the Manual Entry feature.
Expected result: The Phone field should reject non-numeric input and display a validation error (e.g. "Please enter a valid phone number"). The row should not be saveable with letters in the phone field.
Actual result: The phone value "abcDEFghi" is accepted and saved without any validation error. The row is included in the import, and the member is created in the Audience with "abcDEFghi" stored as their phone number.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Navigate to Audience > Import Members > Manual Entry
2. Add any contact with a valid name and email, click + Add
3. Click ✏️ on the row to enter inline edit mode
4. In the Phone field, type alphabetic characters (e.g. "abcDEFghi")
5. Click 💾 to save
6. Click 'Import 1 Members'

Bug title: Duplicate contacts not detected in Import Members > Manual Entry
Notes:
Same root cause as KYPRO-31 (CSV duplicate rows not flagged). Both bugs stem from missing deduplication logic in the import pipeline. Consider a unified fix across all import surfaces.

[2026-04-08] Cross-audience deduplication is also absent. The importer does not check whether an email being imported already exists among current Audience members. This creates silent duplicate records across import sessions and is a higher-impact variant of the intra-batch duplicate issue.
Expected result: The app should detect the duplicate by email and either block the second entry with an inline error ("This email is already in the list"), or flag the row in the Notes column as a duplicate — consistent with the expected behaviour for CSV imports.
Actual result:
Both rows appear in the queue with identical name and email. No duplicate warning, highlight, or Notes annotation is shown. The import counter increments for both.

[NEW - confirmed 2026-04-08] The same missing deduplication applies cross-audience: importing a contact whose email already exists in the Audience (e.g. mailto:alice@example.com, a previously imported member) is accepted without any warning or conflict indicator. The duplicate is silently imported and a second member record with the same email appears in the Audience list.
Steps to reproduce:
Scenario A – Intra-batch duplicate (original):
1. Navigate to Audience > Import Members > Manual Entry
2. Enter a contact – e.g. Name: "Alice Tester", Email: mailto:alice@test.com – and click + Add
3. Enter the exact same name and email again
4. Click + Add again
5. Observe: both rows appear with no duplicate warning

Scenario B – Cross-audience duplicate (confirmed 2026-04-08):
1. Ensure a member with email mailto:alice@example.com already exists in the Audience
2. Navigate to Audience > Import Members > Manual Entry
3. Enter Name: "Alice Johnson", Email: mailto:alice@example.com
4. Click + Add, then click 'Import 1 Members'
5. Observe: import succeeds silently; a second member with the same email appears in Audience

Bug title: Invalid email format accepted in Import Members > Manual Entry
Notes:
The standalone Add Member dialog does validate email format (see KYPRO-39), creating an inconsistency between the two manual-add surfaces. Also relates to KYPRO-29–32 (file imports also lack email validation), though those are tracked separately.

[2026-04-08] Also confirmed in the inline edit flow of the preview table (Path B). Editing an existing row and saving an invalid email is accepted without any error. Same root cause — no client-side email format validation on the preview table's edit mode.
Expected result: An inline validation error should appear on the Email field (e.g. "Please enter a valid email address") and the row should not be added to the queue.
Actual result: The row is added to the import queue with the invalid email intact. No error or warning is shown. Clicking "Import N Members" persists the invalid email to the database. Confirmed by "Invalid Email User" with "not-an-email" visible in the Audience member list.
Steps to reproduce:
Path A – Add Member form (original):
1. Navigate to Audience
2. Click Import Members
3. Select Manual Entry
4. Enter any name (e.g. "Bob Reviewer")
5. Enter an invalid email such as "notanemail" (no @ symbol)
6. Leave Phone and Tags empty
7. Click + Add

Path B – Inline edit of existing preview row (confirmed 2026-04-08):
1. Navigate to Audience > Import Members > Manual Entry
2. Add a valid contact and click + Add
3. Click ✏️ on the row to enter inline edit mode
4. Clear the Email field and type an invalid string (e.g. "notanemail")
5. Click 💾 to save
6. Observe: row is saved with invalid email, no validation error shown

Bug title: Empty Manual Entry form submission silently creates blank null records with no validation feedback
Notes:
Originally assessed as Low severity (no data corrupted, just silent UI failure). Updated after live reproduction: the bug is worse than initially documented — each empty click creates a null '–' record visible in the queue and counted in Preview (N). These null records can propagate into the import. Severity raised to Medium.

[2026-04-08] Further confirmed via inline edit path (see Path B in steps). This path produces an even more severe consequence: a ghost 'Unknown User' is created in the audience and their profile page permanently fails to load (infinite loading skeleton at /members/imported-XXXXXX-0). Severity should be considered for upgrade to High.
Expected result: Clicking Add Member with all fields empty should display a clear validation message indicating that at least Name (or Name + Email) must be filled. The form should reject the submission and not create any record. The member queue should remain unchanged.
Actual result:
Clicking Add Member with all fields empty does NOT simply do nothing — it silently creates a blank '–' record in the member queue. Each click on Add Member adds another null entry. These blank records are then included in the Preview count and can be imported, creating completely empty contacts in the system with no name, email, phone, or tags.

[NEW - confirmed 2026-04-08] The same missing validation applies to the inline edit flow in the preview table: clicking ✏️ on any row, clearing all fields, and pressing 💾 also silently saves the empty row with no error. When that empty row is then imported, it creates a ghost 'Unknown User' member in the Audience. Navigating to that member's profile results in a permanently broken page — a loading skeleton that never resolves (URL: /members/imported-XXXXXX-0). The page never loads and the member is inaccessible.
Steps to reproduce:
Path A – Add Member form:
1. Go to Integrations > Import > Manual Entry
2. Leave all fields blank (Name, Email, Phone, Tags)
3. Click Add Member
4. Click Import

Path B – Inline edit (confirmed 2026-04-08):
1. Go to Audience > Import Members > Manual Entry
2. Add any valid contact and click + Add
3. Click ✏️ on the row to enter edit mode
4. Clear Name, Email, Phone, and Tags fields
5. Click 💾 to save
6. Click 'Import N Members'
7. Observe the resulting member profile
